---
title: "Scenario: Multi-omics Data Integration"
description: >
  Manage and integrate transcriptomic, proteomic, and epigenomic data with full provenance
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

Multi-omics integration combines data from different molecular layers (transcriptomics, proteomics, epigenomics, metabolomics). Each layer undergoes independent preprocessing, and the integration step must track exactly which version of each layer was used.

OmicsLake's lineage tracking captures these cross-layer dependencies automatically through dplyr joins and manually through `depends_on`.

## Setup

```r
library(OmicsLake)
lake <- Lake$new("multiomics_project")
```

## Layer 1: Transcriptomics (RNA-seq)

```r
# Import RNA-seq counts
rnaseq_counts <- data.frame(
  gene_id = paste0("GENE_", 1:5000),
  S1 = rpois(5000, 500), S2 = rpois(5000, 500),
  S3 = rpois(5000, 450), S4 = rpois(5000, 550)
)
lake$put("rnaseq_raw", rnaseq_counts)

# Normalize
dds <- DESeq2::DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = as.matrix(rnaseq_counts[,-1]),
  colData = data.frame(sample = c("S1","S2","S3","S4"),
                        condition = c("A","A","B","B")),
  design = ~condition
)
normalized <- as.data.frame(DESeq2::counts(
  DESeq2::estimateSizeFactors(dds), normalized = TRUE))
normalized$gene_id <- rnaseq_counts$gene_id

lake$put("rnaseq_normalized", normalized, depends_on = "rnaseq_raw")
lake$snap("rnaseq_done")
```

## Layer 2: Proteomics

```r
# Import protein quantification
proteomics <- data.frame(
  protein_id = paste0("PROT_", 1:2000),
  gene_id = paste0("GENE_", sample(1:5000, 2000)),
  S1 = rnorm(2000, 10, 2), S2 = rnorm(2000, 10, 2),
  S3 = rnorm(2000, 9.5, 2), S4 = rnorm(2000, 10.5, 2)
)
lake$put("proteomics_raw", proteomics)

# Filter: remove low-confidence proteins
lake$ref("proteomics_raw") |>
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::if_all(S1:S4, ~ . > 5)) |>
  save_as("proteomics_filtered", lake)

lake$snap("proteomics_done")
```

## Layer 3: Methylation (Epigenomics)

```r
# Import methylation beta values
methylation <- data.frame(
  cpg_id = paste0("cg", sprintf("%08d", 1:10000)),
  gene_id = paste0("GENE_", sample(1:5000, 10000, replace = TRUE)),
  S1 = runif(10000), S2 = runif(10000),
  S3 = runif(10000), S4 = runif(10000)
)
lake$put("methylation_raw", methylation)

# Filter variable CpGs
lake$ref("methylation_raw") |>
  dplyr::rowwise() |>
  dplyr::mutate(var = var(c(S1, S2, S3, S4))) |>
  dplyr::ungroup() |>
  dplyr::filter(var > 0.05) |>
  dplyr::select(-var) |>
  save_as("methylation_variable", lake)

lake$snap("methylation_done")
```

## Layer 4: Metabolomics

```r
# Import metabolite intensities
metabolomics <- data.frame(
  metabolite_id = paste0("MET_", 1:500),
  pathway = sample(c("glycolysis", "tca_cycle", "amino_acid", "lipid"), 500, replace = TRUE),
  S1 = rlnorm(500, 8, 1), S2 = rlnorm(500, 8, 1),
  S3 = rlnorm(500, 7.5, 1), S4 = rlnorm(500, 8.5, 1)
)
lake$put("metabolomics_raw", metabolomics)

# Log-transform and normalize
lake$ref("metabolomics_raw") |>
  dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(S1:S4, log2)) |>
  save_as("metabolomics_normalized", lake)

lake$snap("metabolomics_done")
```

## Integration: Transcriptomics × Proteomics

```r
# Join RNA and protein data by gene_id (auto-tracked!)
lake$ref("rnaseq_normalized") |>
  dplyr::inner_join(
    lake$ref("proteomics_filtered") |>
      dplyr::select(gene_id,
                    prot_S1 = S1, prot_S2 = S2,
                    prot_S3 = S3, prot_S4 = S4),
    by = "gene_id"
  ) |>
  save_as("rna_protein_integrated", lake)

lake$tree("rna_protein_integrated")
# rna_protein_integrated
#   ├── rnaseq_normalized
#   │     └── rnaseq_raw
#   └── proteomics_filtered
#         └── proteomics_raw
```

## Integration: All Four Layers

```r
# Summarize methylation per gene
lake$ref("methylation_variable") |>
  dplyr::group_by(gene_id) |>
  dplyr::summarize(
    mean_meth_S1 = mean(S1), mean_meth_S2 = mean(S2),
    mean_meth_S3 = mean(S3), mean_meth_S4 = mean(S4),
    n_cpgs = dplyr::n()
  ) |>
  save_as("methylation_per_gene", lake)

# Full integration
lake$ref("rna_protein_integrated") |>
  dplyr::left_join(lake$ref("methylation_per_gene"), by = "gene_id") |>
  save_as("full_integrated", lake)

lake$snap("integration_v1")
```

## Cross-layer Lineage

```r
lake$tree("full_integrated")
# full_integrated
#   ├── rna_protein_integrated
#   │     ├── rnaseq_normalized
#   │     │     └── rnaseq_raw
#   │     └── proteomics_filtered
#   │           └── proteomics_raw
#   └── methylation_per_gene
#         └── methylation_variable
#               └── methylation_raw
```

Every layer's preprocessing is tracked. You can trace any integrated result back to its raw sources.

## Versioned Integration Iterations

```r
# Try alternative integration (intersection only)
lake$ref("rna_protein_integrated") |>
  dplyr::inner_join(lake$ref("methylation_per_gene"), by = "gene_id") |>
  save_as("strict_integrated", lake)

lake$snap("integration_v2_strict")

# Compare
dim(lake$get("full_integrated"))    # More genes (left join)
dim(lake$get("strict_integrated"))  # Fewer genes (inner join)
```

## Release Manifest

```r
# Document which versions were used
ol_commit(
  note = "Multi-omics integration v1 for manuscript",
  params = list(
    rnaseq_snapshot = "rnaseq_done",
    proteomics_snapshot = "proteomics_done",
    methylation_snapshot = "methylation_done",
    metabolomics_snapshot = "metabolomics_done",
    integration_method = "left_join by gene_id"
  )
)

# Export for collaborators
lake$export("full_integrated", "results/integrated_multiomics.parquet")
```
